title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for due scheduling, monotonic clocks, backoff, jitter, and pause policy and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deterministic adaptive polling scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher hints, pause state, and scheduling reason independently from process memory.
Inject wall and monotonic time roles, define inclusive due comparisons, and test forward, backward, and boundary changes.
Apply bounded exponential backoff with deterministic-testable jitter, reset rules, Retry-After, and maximum staleness escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Pause Resume
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 1586 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Pause Resume
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Pause Resume: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 5015 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Pause Resume.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.
